name: Stem Cell Rejuvenation Coverage Repair Assessment for June 9, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage repair created after the June 9 surveillance assessment requested active registry, review triangulation, formal anchor, and taxonomy-boundary repair before ordinary surveillance resumed.

summary: Coverage is now adequate for the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, pending review and publication of the staged repair bundle. The repair stages hDNAgr therapy, NCT06877377 no-results registry context, and a recent HSC aging review while explicitly excluding older withdrawn/no-results registries and adjacent 15-PGDH, MMP9, mitochondrial, and bone-marrow stromal sources from the public track. Coverage is not strong because the human intervention layer remains no-results or biomarker-level, safety and durability are unresolved, and formal citation-network auditing remains incomplete.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages NCT06877377 as no-results targeted HSC-aging registry context and keeps the existing exercise satellite-cell biomarker anchor. This is adequate for a low-confidence human_biomarker_signal claim, but not for human functional benefit.
evidence_categories.gap_note: The remaining problem is an evidence gap: there are still no posted targeted human HSC-rejuvenation results.

evidence_categories.rationale: The direct human HSC Cdc42/CASIN record is ex vivo and supports target biology, while PMID 42183054 was excluded as adjacent bone-marrow stromal context. Observational human HSC aging and clonality coverage remains incomplete but is not required for the current public claim.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A future stronger coverage pass could map human HSC aging, clonality, and biopsy-based stem-cell aging studies.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes systemic CASIN aged-mouse lifespan support plus animal Cdc42, NAD, and p38 functional support. The repair explicitly excluded adjacent same-window MMP9 and 15-PGDH animal tissue-repair sources from public staging to preserve taxonomy clarity.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A stronger verdict would require broader formal animal-source triangulation across tissue classes.

evidence_categories.rationale: Cdc42 polarity, NAD-linked mitochondrial function, p38 MAPK signaling, exercise-linked Pax7 satellite-cell movement, systemic CASIN effects, hDNAgr registry endpoints, and the HSC review provide enough mechanistic coverage for the current public claim.

The repair stages no-results hDNAgr registry context and records older withdrawn or no-results registry leads as exclusions, while the outlook preserves no treated-human benefit and safety-boundary language.
The staged hDNAgr registry has safety as the primary endpoint and the outlook names proliferative, genomic, telomere, and lineage-skewing risks, but no posted chronic safety or durability data exist.
evidence_categories.gap_note: This remains an evidence gap requiring future results, not another immediate coverage-repair pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The high-priority active-registry gap is repaired by staging NCT06877377. Older NCT03473288 and NCT03754842 were rechecked and explicitly excluded or deferred as withdrawn/no-results or NAD-overlap context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Watch NCT06877377 during ordinary surveillance for recruitment, status changes, posted results, or linked publications.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair stages PMID 42216078 as a direct HSC aging and rejuvenation strategy review, and records adjacent mitochondrial, neural-crest, and bone-marrow stromal records as boundary context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: A deeper citation-network audit could improve strong coverage, but it is not required before ordinary surveillance.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline already includes obvious Cdc42/CASIN, systemic CASIN, NAD, p38, and human exercise/Pax7 anchors; the repair adds a direct HSC review to triangulate those anchors without finding a missing must-stage primary anchor.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Citation-network auditing remains optional future work rather than an immediate blocker.

evidence_categories.rationale: The repair keeps hDNAgr inside Stem Cell Rejuvenation because it explicitly targets HSC aging, while excluding 15-PGDH muscle repair, MMP9 aged-bone repair, mitochondrial stem-cell aging, and bone-marrow stromal mobilization sources from public staging to avoid collapsing adjacent tissue-repair, immune, mitochondrial, and niche-support claims.

search_log_summary: The coverage repair directly rechecked NCT06877377, NCT03473288, and NCT03754842 through ClinicalTrials.gov API v2; sampled recent PubMed reviews from 2024 through June 9, 2026; selected PMID 42216078 as direct HSC review context; and excluded adjacent 15-PGDH, MMP9, mitochondrial, neural-crest, and bone-marrow stromal records from the public track.
source_selection_notes: NCT06877377 was staged because it directly targets HSC aging and repaired the high-priority active-registry gap.
PMID 42216078 was staged because it directly reviews HSC aging mechanisms and rejuvenation strategies.
NCT03473288 was excluded because it was withdrawn with zero enrollment.
NCT03754842 was excluded from public staging because it is NR/pterostilbene muscle-regeneration context and belongs closer to NAD overlap unless results appear.
PMID 42228536 and PMID 42220642 were not staged because their main decision value is taxonomy-boundary context.

known_gaps.description: The repaired map includes targeted hDNAgr registry context, but no result-bearing targeted human HSC rejuvenation study is available.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During ordinary surveillance, recheck NCT06877377 for recruitment, posted results, or linked publications before changing the public stage.

known_gaps.description: No staged source establishes safety, durability, proliferation risk, lineage-skewing risk, tumor risk, genomic risk, or telomere-risk boundaries for targeted human stem-cell rejuvenation interventions.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public read low-confidence until result-bearing targeted human studies report safety and efficacy-relevant endpoints.

known_gaps.description: The current anchors are plausible and review-triangulated, but no formal citation-network audit has been recorded.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run a deeper citation-network or must-include audit only if a future public stage change is contemplated.

next_coverage_action: Resume ordinary surveillance after review and publication of the staged coverage-repair bundle. Watch NCT06877377 for recruitment, status changes, posted results, or linked publications, and keep adjacent 15-PGDH/MMP9 tissue-repair sources out of this track unless taxonomy review says otherwise.
